title: Segregate odd and even nodes in Linked List

content.problemStatement:
Given the head of a singly linked list. Group all the nodes with odd indices followed by all the nodes with even indices and return the reordered list.



Consider the 1st node to have index 1 and so on. The relative order of the elements inside the odd and even group must remain the same as the given input.
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the linked list <= 10^5
content.inputFormat: The input is the head of a singly linked list where each node contains an integer value.
content.outputFormat: The output is the head of the modified linked list where all nodes with odd indices are followed by all nodes with even indices.

sampleTestCases.output:
2→4→6→1→3→5→Null
**Explanation :** Odd Nodes in LinkedList are 1,3,5 and Even Nodes in LinkedList are 2,4,6
In Modified LinkedList all even Nodes comes before all Odd Nodes. So Modified LinkedList looks like 2→4→6→1→3→5→Null. Order of even and odd Nodes is 
maintained in modified LinkedList.

sampleTestCases.explanation: As there are no Even Nodes in LinkedList, The Modified LinkedList is same as Original LinkedList.

solution.approach: Create two separate linked lists for odd and even nodes, then combine them.

code.Python3:
# Node definition for singly-linked list
class Node:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data
        self.next = None

class Solution:

    # Function to segregate even and odd nodes in a linked list
    def segregateEvenOdd(self, head):

        # Edge case: If list is empty or has only one node
        if head is None or head.next is None:
            return head

        # Create pointers for the heads and tails of even and odd lists
        odd = head
        even = head.next
        evenHead = even

        # Traverse the linked list
        while even is not None and even.next is not None:
            odd.next = even.next
            odd = odd.next
            even.next = odd.next
            even = even.next

        # Combine even and odd lists
        odd.next = evenHead

        return head

# Driver code
def printList(head):
    while head:
        print(head.data, end=" ")
        head = head.next

head = Node(1)
head.next = Node(2)
head.next.next = Node(3)
head.next.next.next = Node(4)
head.next.next.next.next = None

sol = Solution()
newHead = sol.segregateEvenOdd(head)
printList(newHead)
